title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who traveled abroad to learn a traditional skill. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: The Global Classroom: Learning Across Borders

texts.A:
### Tom, Muay Thai in Thailand
'I went there thinking I was fit, but the first session humbled me,' Tom admits. [47]The humidity was a physical weight that I hadn't accounted for in my training plans.[/47] [53]I learned that martial arts is less about fighting and more about the discipline of the ego.[/53] [51]The language barrier in the gym was tough, but the universal language of sweat and effort bridged the gap.[/51] [54]I was amazed by the generosity of the local fighters who took the time to correct my stance despite their own grueling schedules.[/54] [50]I found that the mental toughness I developed helped me navigate a difficult period in my personal life later on.[/50] [56]If I hadn't won a travel scholarship, I never could have afforded the three months of intensive training.[/56] It was a grueling but essential journey.
texts.B:
### Elena, Perfumery in France
Elena spent a summer in Grasse. 'My sense of smell was completely overwhelmed at first,' she says. [48]I was surprised to find that my allergy to certain flowers actually improved as I learned more about natural essences.[/48] [52]I hadn't anticipated the chemistry involved; it’s far more scientific than the romantic art I’d imagined.[/52] [49]The trip taught me to slow down and appreciate the subtle layers of the world around me.[/49] [55]It took weeks of trial and error before I could identify individual notes in a complex blend without help.[/55] [50]I’ve found that the precision required in blending has made me much more organized in my professional life as a designer.[/50] [47]The intense heat of the fields during harvest was a shock to my system.[/47] It was a sensory awakening.
texts.C:
### Sofia, Flamenco in Spain
'I wanted to understand the soul of the dance,' Sofia explains. [51]The most difficult part was the cultural nuances; you can't just learn the steps, you have to feel the history.[/51] [48]I noticed that my chronic foot pain vanished as my technique improved and I learned how to move correctly.[/48] [54]I was moved by the way the local community embraced me, an outsider, once they saw my dedication.[/54] [52]I’d initially worried that I was too old to start such a physically demanding dance, but the older dancers were the most inspiring.[/52] [49]The experience gave me a new way to express emotions that I’d previously kept bottled up.[/49] [53]I realized that flamenco is a form of resistance and resilience, not just entertainment.[/53] It was a profound emotional release.
texts.D:
### Marcus, Traditional Woodworking in Japan
Marcus learned 'shou sugi ban' and joinery. 'The precision required is terrifying,' he notes. [55]I spent the first two weeks just sharpening my tools, which was incredibly frustrating for someone used to power tools.[/55] [47]The cold in the unheated mountain workshop was a constant challenge that I hadn't really prepared for.[/47] [53]The master taught me that a mistake isn't a failure, but a chance to change the design.[/53] [50]I’ve noticed that the patience I learned at the workbench has made me a much better listener in my daily life.[/50] [56]I wouldn't have found the apprenticeship if I hadn't spent a year learning the basics of the language beforehand.[/56] [49]It changed my relationship with objects; I now value quality over quantity.[/49] It was a masterclass in mindfulness.

questions.question: mentions being unprepared for the specific climatic conditions of their training?
questions.explanation: Tom (A) mentions humidity, Elena (B) mentions the heat of the fields, and Marcus (D) mentions the cold workshop.

questions.question: describes an unexpected improvement in a health-related issue?
questions.explanation: Elena (B) mentions her allergy improved, and Sofia (C) mentions her foot pain vanished.

questions.question: expresses how the skill has provided a new way of interacting with the world?
questions.explanation: Elena (B) learned to appreciate subtle layers, Sofia (C) found a way to express emotions, and Marcus (D) values quality over quantity.

questions.question: notes that the discipline learned has had a positive impact on their character or work?
questions.explanation: Tom (A) mentions mental toughness helping in personal life, Elena (B) is more organized, and Marcus (D) is a better listener.

questions.question: mentions a difficulty related to communication or cultural understanding?
questions.explanation: Tom (A) mentions the language barrier, and Sofia (C) mentions cultural nuances and history.

questions.question: admits to having had prior misconceptions or doubts about the skill or themselves?
questions.explanation: Elena (B) thought it was a romantic art (not scientific), and Sofia (C) thought she was too old.

questions.question: reflects on a deeper philosophical lesson taught by their instructor or the craft?
questions.explanation: Tom (A) learned about the discipline of the ego, and Marcus (D) learned that mistakes are chances to change design.

questions.question: mentions the kindness or support of people they met during their stay?
questions.explanation: Tom (A) mentions the generosity of local fighters, and Sofia (C) mentions the community embracing her.

questions.question: describes a period of frustration before any real progress was made?
questions.explanation: Elena (B) spent weeks on trial and error, and Marcus (D) spent two weeks just sharpening tools.

questions.question: acknowledges the importance of a specific opportunity or preparation that allowed the trip to happen?
questions.explanation: Tom (A) mentions a scholarship, and Marcus (D) mentions learning the language beforehand.
